title: Database queries should not be vulnerable to injection attacks
description: Database injections (such as SQL injections) occur in an application when the application retrieves data from a user or a third-party service and inserts it into a database query without sanitizing it first. If an application contains a database query that is vulnerable to injections, it is exposed to attacks that target any database where that query is used. A user with malicious intent is able to modify the existing query to change its logic to a malicious one.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detect SQL execution methods with string concatenation or interpolation instead of parameterized queries
logic.triggering_conditions: (SQL execution method call AND (string concatenation with + OR string interpolation $"" OR string.Format)) AND NOT parameterized
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude logging, tests, comments, and already-parameterized queries
logic.reasoning: SQL injection occurs when user input is concatenated directly into SQL queries. This rule detects dangerous patterns: ExecuteSqlRaw/FromSqlRaw/ExecuteSqlCommand with string concatenation (+), interpolation ($""), or string.Format. Excludes parameterized queries (SqlParameter), LINQ queries, logging, and tests. High severity requires compound checks to minimize false positives.

should_trigger: string query = "SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Id = '" + userId + "'"; context.Database.ExecuteSqlRaw(query);
var query = $"SELECT * FROM Products WHERE Name = '{searchTerm}'"; context.Products.FromSqlRaw(query);
var sql = string.Format("DELETE FROM {0} WHERE Id = {1}", tableName, id); context.Database.ExecuteSqlCommand(sql);
command.CommandText = "SELECT * FROM " + tableName + " WHERE Id = " + id; command.ExecuteReader();
should_not_trigger: string query = "SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Id = @userId"; context.Database.ExecuteSqlRaw(query, new SqlParameter("@userId", userId));
var user = context.Users.Find(userId); user.Name = name; context.SaveChanges();
[Test] public void TestQuery() { var sql = "SELECT * FROM " + table; }

checks.message: SQL injection vulnerability: query uses string concatenation/interpolation instead of parameterized queries
